 {"id":"bd-iic1","title":"Phase 2.2: Switch bdt storage to TOON format","description":"Currently bdt stores issues in JSONL format in issues.toon file. Phase 2.2 must implement actual TOON format storage - this is the fundamental goal of the bdtoon project.

## Current State (Phase 2.1)
- issues.toon stores JSONL (intermediate format)
- --toon flag allows output in TOON format for LLM consumption
- Problem: We're not actually using TOON as the fundamental storage format

## Required Work (Phase 2.2)
1. Switch issue file I/O to write TOON format instead of JSONL
   - Update cmd/bdt/storage.go to use EncodeTOON for writing
   - Update cmd/bdt/storage.go to decode TOON (currently decodes JSON)
   - Ensure round-trip: write TOON → read TOON → write TOON is byte-identical

2. Update command implementations
   - cmd/bdt/create.go: Write newly created issues to TOON format
   - cmd/bdt/list.go: Read issues from TOON format
   - cmd/bdt/show.go: Read from TOON format
   - cmd/bdt/import.go: Convert imported JSONL to TOON
   - cmd/bdt/export.go: Export TOON to JSONL (for bd compatibility)

3. Implement TOON parser that handles gotoon's encoder-only limitation
   - Since gotoon doesn't decode TOON, need custom TOON→JSON decoder
   - OR continue storing TOON but decoding via intermediate JSON conversion

4. Git merge driver optimization
   - TOON is line-oriented, better for 3-way merges than binary formats
   - Configure git merge driver for .toon files

5. Comprehensive testing
   - Round-trip tests: Issue → TOON → storage → read → Issue
   - Merge conflict resolution tests with TOON format
   - Large issue set performance tests

## Success Criteria
- issues.toon stores actual TOON format (not JSONL)
- bdt list reads from TOON file
- bdt create writes to TOON file
- Round-trip: create issue → list → show returns identical data
- All 65+ tests still passing
- Performance comparable to JSONL storage","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:05:41.394964404-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:17.879612634-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:17.879612634-07:00"}

Uses ValidateTemplate() under the hood.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/emma","created_at":"2026-01-01T11:19:09.036754-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip","updated_at":"2026-01-01T14:20:06.932309-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T14:20:06.932309-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented --validate flag for bd create command","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ik1b","depends_on_id":"bd-ou35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-01T11:19:22.754415-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip"},{"issue_id":"bd-ik1b","depends_on_id":"bd-615z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-01T11:19:23.964595-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip"}]}
-{"id":"bd-imxi","title":"Add bd setup gemini for Gemini CLI integration","description":"## Summary

Add `bd setup gemini` command to install beads hooks for Gemini CLI, similar to existing `bd setup claude`.

Ref: GitHub #845

## Research Findings

### Gemini CLI Has Compatible Hooks

Gemini CLI (https://geminicli.com/docs/hooks/) supports nearly identical hook events:

| Gemini CLI Event | Claude Code Equivalent | Purpose |
|------------------|----------------------|---------|
| **SessionStart** | SessionStart | Initialize resources, load context |
| **SessionEnd** | - | Clean up, save state |
| **PreCompress** | PreCompact | Before context compression |
| **BeforeAgent** | - | Add context before planning |

Configuration format is almost identical to Claude:
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [{
      "matcher": "",
      "hooks": [{
        "type": "command",
        "command": "bd prime"
      }]
    }]
  }
}
```

### GEMINI.md Support

- Hierarchical loading: ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md → project root → subdirectories
- Concatenated into system prompt automatically
- Gemini CLI also reads AGENTS.md (already supported via `bd setup factory`)

### Antigravity

Google's IDE-based agentic platform (released Nov 2025):
- Uses Gemini 3 with 1M token context
- Has "knowledge base" for saved context
- Less documented - may share Gemini CLI infrastructure
- Needs separate research for dedicated support

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Core Gemini CLI Support

1. **Create `cmd/bd/setup/gemini.go`**
   - Mirror structure from `claude.go`
   - Target: `~/.gemini/settings.json`
   - Install SessionStart and PreCompress hooks
   - Support --project flag for local settings
   - Support --stealth flag

2. **Add doctor check in `cmd/bd/doctor/gemini.go`**
   - Check if hooks installed
   - Verify settings.json structure

3. **Update `cmd/bd/setup.go`**
   - Add gemini subcommand
   - Wire up to setup/gemini.go

### Phase 2: Optional Enhancements

4. **GEMINI.md generation** (optional)
   - Could generate project-level GEMINI.md
   - Or document that AGENTS.md already works

5. **Antigravity support** (separate issue)
   - Needs research on Antigravity's configuration system
   - May be able to share Gemini CLI hooks
   - May need IDE-specific integration

## Open Questions

1. Where does Gemini CLI store settings? Need to verify `~/.gemini/settings.json` path
2. Does Gemini CLI use same JSON structure as Claude for hooks?
3. Can one implementation cover both Gemini CLI and Antigravity?
4. Should we generate GEMINI.md or rely on AGENTS.md?

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bd setup gemini` installs hooks to Gemini CLI settings
- [ ] `bd setup gemini --check` verifies installation
- [ ] `bd doctor` reports Gemini CLI integration status
- [ ] Documentation updated in docs/SETUP.md","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-01T23:47:07.399783-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/wolf","updated_at":"2026-01-01T23:55:12.69739-08:00","comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-imxi","author":"beads/crew/wolf","text":"Deprioritizing Antigravity - focusing on Gemini CLI only. Antigravity may share the same hook infrastructure anyway, so Gemini CLI support might just work. Will revisit if there's demand.","created_at":"2026-01-02T07:55:13Z"}]}

Add `bd setup gemini` command to install beads hooks for Gemini CLI, similar to existing `bd setup claude`.

Ref: GitHub #845

## Research Findings

### Gemini CLI Has Compatible Hooks

Gemini CLI (https://geminicli.com/docs/hooks/) supports nearly identical hook events:

| Gemini CLI Event | Claude Code Equivalent | Purpose |
|------------------|----------------------|---------|
| **SessionStart** | SessionStart | Initialize resources, load context |
| **SessionEnd** | - | Clean up, save state |
| **PreCompress** | PreCompact | Before context compression |
| **BeforeAgent** | - | Add context before planning |

Configuration format is almost identical to Claude:

  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [{
      "matcher": "",

        "type": "command",
        "command": "bd prime"

```

- Hierarchical loading: ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md → project root → subdirectories
- Concatenated into system prompt automatically
- Gemini CLI also reads AGENTS.md (already supported via `bd setup factory`)

### Antigravity

Google's IDE-based agentic platform (released Nov 2025):
- Uses Gemini 3 with 1M token context
- Has "knowledge base" for saved context
- Less documented - may share Gemini CLI infrastructure
- Needs separate research for dedicated support

### Phase 1: Core Gemini CLI Support

   - Mirror structure from `claude.go`
   - Target: `~/.gemini/settings.json`

   - Support --project flag for local settings
   - Support --stealth flag

2. **Add doctor check in `cmd/bd/doctor/gemini.go`**

3. **Update `cmd/bd/setup.go`**
   - Add gemini subcommand

4. **GEMINI.md generation** (optional)
   - Could generate project-level GEMINI.md
   - Or document that AGENTS.md already works

5. **Antigravity support** (separate issue)
   - Needs research on Antigravity's configuration system
   - May be able to share Gemini CLI hooks
   - May need IDE-specific integration

## Open Questions

1. Where does Gemini CLI store settings? Need to verify `~/.gemini/settings.json` path
2. Does Gemini CLI use same JSON structure as Claude for hooks?
3. Can one implementation cover both Gemini CLI and Antigravity?
4. Should we generate GEMINI.md or rely on AGENTS.md?

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bd setup gemini` installs hooks to Gemini CLI settings
- [ ] `bd setup gemini --check` verifies installation
- [ ] `bd doctor` reports Gemini CLI integration status

 {"id":"bd-indn","title":"bd template commands fail with daemon mode","description":"The `bd template show` and `bd template instantiate` commands fail with 'Error loading template: no database connection' when daemon is running.

**Reproduction:**
```bash
bd daemon --start
bd template show bd-qqc  # Error: no database connection
bd template show bd-qqc --no-daemon  # Works
```

**Expected:** Template commands should work with daemon like other commands.

**Workaround:** Use `--no-daemon` flag.

**Location:** Likely in cmd/bd/template.go - daemon RPC path not implemented for template operations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T22:57:35.16596-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T22:40:32.763595-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T22:40:32.763595-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-io8c","title":"Improve test coverage for internal/syncbranch (33.0% → 70%)","description":"Improve test coverage for internal/syncbranch package from 27% to 70%.

## Current State
- Coverage: 27.0%
- Files: syncbranch.go, worktree.go
- Tests: syncbranch_test.go (basic tests exist)

## Functions Needing Tests

### syncbranch.go (config management)
- [x] ValidateBranchName - has tests
- [ ] Get - needs store mock tests
- [ ] GetFromYAML - needs YAML parsing tests
- [ ] IsConfigured - needs file system tests
- [ ] IsConfiguredWithDB - needs DB path tests
- [ ] Set - needs store mock tests
- [ ] Unset - needs store mock tests

### worktree.go (git operations) - PRIORITY
- [ ] CommitToSyncBranch - needs git repo fixture tests
- [ ] PullFromSyncBranch - needs merge scenario tests
- [ ] CheckDivergence - needs ahead/behind tests
- [ ] ResetToRemote - needs reset scenario tests
- [ ] performContentMerge - needs 3-way merge tests
- [ ] extractJSONLFromCommit - needs git show tests
- [ ] hasChangesInWorktree - needs dirty state tests
- [ ] commitInWorktree - needs commit scenario tests

## Implementation Guide

1. **Use testutil fixtures:**
   ```go
   import "github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/testutil/fixtures"
   
   func TestCommitToSyncBranch(t *testing.T) {
       repo := fixtures.NewGitRepo(t)
       defer repo.Cleanup()
       // ... test scenarios
   }
   ```

2. **Test scenarios for worktree.go:**
   - Clean commit (no conflicts)
   - Non-fast-forward push (diverged)
   - Merge conflict resolution
   - Empty changes (nothing to commit)

3. **Mock storage for syncbranch.go:**
   ```go
   store := memory.New()
   // Set up test config
   syncbranch.Set(ctx, store, "beads-sync")
   ```

## Success Criteria
- Coverage ≥ 70%
- All public functions have at least one test
- Edge cases covered for git operations
- Tests pass with `go test -race ./internal/syncbranch`

## Run Tests
```bash
go test -v -cover ./internal/syncbranch
go test -race ./internal/syncbranch
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T20:43:02.079145-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:46:10.191435-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:46:10.191435-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-io8c","depends_on_id":"bd-iz5t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:44:07.213092-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
